scope.summary: Re-scope and own the backend work that rode into recovery commit 3bf307a8 under MT-101 whose contract forbids src/backend/**. Owns: (1) validate_native_workspace_model_launch_contract in workflows.rs - marker-gated fail-closed validation of native workspace model_run launches (launch_surface=handshake_native, launch_mode=workspace_model_session, session_id/wrapper required, workspace_folder==working_dir, provider==backend) plus 7 pure unit tests; (2) api/jobs.rs model_run lane changes incl. workflow_run pre-creation and legacy null-binding repair; (3) managed_postgres.rs remediation - default pgdata under ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-product/managed_pgdata, ensure_running wait/adopt when postmaster.pid exists but pg_isready is not yet ready (double pg_ctl start race fix); (4) storage/postgres.rs postgres_test_base_url bootstrap of managed PostgreSQL when POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL absent; (5) managed-PG auto-boot test harness adoption across backend test files plus DuckDbFlightRecorder to NoopTestRecorder swap in workflows tests; (6) session worktrees default to ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-tool/session_worktrees. GLOBAL-SCOPE-005 disclosure: this work was performed by the prior session as cross-cutting PostgreSQL recovery attached to the MT-101 continuation, violating MT-101 forbidden_paths; this MT is the governed re-scope so the Operator has one place to waive or reject it. Full touched-file list is enumerated in commit 3bf307a8 message and packet status_sync notes.

acceptance_criteria.text: Launch-contract validator fail-closes malformed native workspace launches: 7 pure unit tests (native_workspace_model_run_*) green without PostgreSQL; legacy non-native launches keep UUID fallback.

Managed PostgreSQL bootstraps by default when POSTGRES_TEST_URL and DATABASE_URL are absent: postgres_test_base_url_bootstraps_default_postgres_when_url_candidates_missing green executing SELECT 1 against real managed PG; pgdata under ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-product/managed_pgdata; no repo-local managed_pgdata residue.

PG-bound model_run lifecycle behaviors proven against real managed PostgreSQL: model_run_timeout_ms_fails_before_simulated_runtime_finishes; model_run_dispatch_repairs_legacy_null_workflow_run_before_parse_failure; model_run_startup_repair_backfills_legacy_null_workflow_run; model_run_capability_denial_marks_precreated_workflow_failed.
from_env_defaults_are_disk_agnostic green; no drive-specific absolute paths introduced in defaults.
Operator waiver disposition recorded for the forbidden-path breach that this MT re-scopes (why plus full touched list), per GLOBAL-SCOPE-005 / HBR-STOP.

gui_obligation.not_applicable_reason: Backend-only re-scope: launch-contract validation and managed-PG test harness have no operator-visible surface; native launch UI is MT-101 scope. Trace evidence lives in typed WorkflowError rejections and test output.

user_manual_obligation.not_applicable_reason: Operator-facing behavior unchanged: the validator only rejects malformed programmatic launches (native UI always emits the valid shape). Manual coverage of model-session launch semantics is owned by MT-104; the backend user_manual/seed.rs diff rode along in 3bf307a8 and is inspected under MT-104 obligations.

risks: Re-scoped work was authored under a violated contract; silent acceptance without waiver normalizes forbidden-path drift.
Managed-PG adoption path could mask a genuinely broken pg_ctl start by waiting forever.
Test-harness auto-boot could leak postgres processes across parallel WP sessions on a loaded host.
minimum_controls: AC-107-5 waiver disposition is mandatory before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.
ensure_running adoption is bounded by pg_isready timeout, proven by rerun of the no-env bootstrap test.
Proof runs record pg process ownership; artifact hygiene check confirms no orphan pgdata outside the artifacts root.
